// Network Fundamentals — Tools

CISCO
PACKET TRACER

A free network simulation tool from Cisco for building and testing networks without any physical hardware. This guide tours every part of the Packet Tracer window, then walks you through installing version 7.3.0 on Windows, step by step.

CCNA
Exam-prep friendly
Free
From Cisco NetAcad
9
Window components
01
// What it is

WHAT IS PACKET TRACER?

Cisco Packet Tracer is a program that simulates the operation of network devices — and the equipment connected to a company's network — made by Cisco. It can simulate routers, switches, access points, hubs, computers, servers, and wireless LAN controllers, along with different connection types such as Ethernet, Serial, and Wireless.

Connecting devices together is fairly easy and uncomplicated, which makes Packet Tracer ideal for people just beginning to study or work in networking. It's especially well suited to anyone preparing for Cisco's Associate-level (CCNA) certification, and it even covers some Professional-level (CCNP) topics.

🧪
Practice
🔍
Discovery
🛠️
Troubleshooting
🎓
Exam prep
No hardware needed: everything runs in software, so you can design, cable, configure, and test a whole network on a single laptop — then watch packets travel across it in slow motion.
02
// What it simulates

DEVICES & CONNECTIONS

Packet Tracer ships with a large library of Cisco gear and generic end devices. Here's the core of what you can drop onto the canvas:

🔀
Routers

Forward packets between networks — 4331, 2911, 1941, 819, PT-Router and more.

🔁
Switches

Connect devices within a LAN; support VLANs, trunking and STP.

📡
Access Points & WLC

Wireless access points and Wireless LAN Controllers for Wi-Fi networks.

💻
End Devices

PCs, laptops, servers, printers, IP phones, and IoT devices.

🔌
Hubs

Legacy Layer-1 repeaters, useful for demonstrating collisions and broadcasts.

🧵
Connections

Ethernet (straight/crossover), Serial, Console, Fiber, and Wireless links.

03
// Anatomy of the interface

Below is the actual Packet Tracer window with its parts numbered. Click any numbered area on the screenshot (or use the buttons below) to learn what each component does. Together these nine parts are everything you interact with while building a network.

04
// Step by step on Windows

HOW TO INSTALL (v7.3.0)

Packet Tracer is developed for Cisco Networking Academy students. Register and download it free from netacad.com. Then follow this interactive walkthrough — click Next step to advance through the installer exactly as it appears on Windows.

📥 Official download & free registration: https://www.netacad.com/courses/packet-tracer  — this guide skips the registration step and starts from the downloaded installer.
1

Step 1 of 11
05
// After installing

FIRST LAUNCH

Once installation finishes, a few things happen the first time you open Packet Tracer. Here's what to expect:

🛡️
Firewall prompt

Windows Defender may block some features. Click Allow access so Packet Tracer can use the network normally.

🔑
Sign in

A NetAcad sign-in screen appears. Log in with your Networking Academy account, or register first if you don't have one.

✅
Ready

The main window opens — installation complete. You're ready to drag your first router onto the canvas.

Guest option: if you don't want to log in every time, the sign-in screen also offers a limited "Guest Login" that lets you use the program after a short wait.
